setting.note: France in the summer of 1940 and the Occupation that follows. Part One drives the panicked exodus out of Paris and down the choked roads south through the Loire; Part Two settles into an occupied Burgundy village where villagers and billeted German soldiers share the same streets. The map runs from the capital to the wine country of Saône-et-Loire.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Irène Némirovsky, written 1942, first published 2004. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Suite Française, Irène Némirovsky (2004). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A muted wartime blue-grey, the colour of the roads south under bombardment and the requisitioned village. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
